question: Should the next ONNX tokenizer work package HF Rust tokenizer integration behind build tags/native packaging, use a sidecar, or defer integration?

options.label: Build-tag integration next
options.tradeoffs: Adds `hf_tokenizers` build-tag path and native library packaging, preserving default TEI builds. More engineering work, but keeps correctness and avoids breaking normal builds.

options.label: Tokenizer sidecar next
options.tradeoffs: Keeps Go builds simpler and uses proven Python/HF tokenization, but adds a process boundary and operational complexity.

options.label: Defer ONNX integration
options.tradeoffs: Stops ONNX work after proving parity path exists. Lowest risk, but leaves performance upside unexplored.

recommendationRationale: The HF Rust binding proved exact token parity. A build-tag/native-packaging milestone can preserve default builds while enabling the opt-in ONNX backend to become semantically correct.
